questions.question: What is the multimodal agent loop for a task like "book me a flight to Tokyo under $800"?
options: One giant prompt that outputs the booking

Perceive (screenshot) → reason (plan from screenshot + URL + goal) → act (emit a structured JSON action like click/type/scroll) → observe the new state → repeat — each step a VLM call, with errors compounding across steps
A web crawler that scrapes all flight data
A fine-tuned classifier per website
questions.explanation: Recovery matters precisely because errors compound. This capstone pulls together every Phase 12 thread: high-res perception, tool-use reasoning, coordinate grounding, long-horizon memory, evaluation.

questions.question: What is GUI grounding, and what established the primitive?
options: Electrically grounding the monitor

Given a screenshot and an instruction, output the (x, y) to click — SeeClick proved it at scale by fine-tuning a VLM to emit coordinates as text; CogAgent added 1120x1120 high-res encoding (~84% web navigation); Ferret-UI took it to mobile
Parsing HTML without rendering it
Recording human mouse movements
questions.explanation: Output is usually JSON like {"action": "click", "x": 384, "y": 220, "element_desc": "Search button"} — the element_desc lets the system re-ground semantically if coordinates drift between screenshots.

questions.question: How do screenshot-only, accessibility-tree, and hybrid input modes compare?
options: They are interchangeable in reliability

Screenshot-only is most general (any app) but unstructured; the accessibility tree grounds far more reliably where available; production agents go hybrid — tree for atomic-action grounding, screenshot for semantic context
Trees are always available everywhere
Screenshots are deprecated in production
questions.explanation: Browser automation always has the tree (Selenium + accessibility); desktop apps sometimes do. The hybrid takes reliability where it can and generality where it must.

questions.question: How do agents keep 20-step workflows from overflowing the VLM context?
options: They just use a bigger model

Compression: summary-chain (summarize every 5 steps, drop old screenshots), skip-frame (keep first/last/every 3rd), or a tool-recorded text log of actions taken — the log pattern, used by Claude's computer-use API, is simpler and more reliable
Each step starts a fresh conversation
Screenshots are stored at 1x1 pixel
questions.explanation: Twenty screenshots of history is mostly redundant pixels. A text log of what was done preserves the decision-relevant state at a fraction of the tokens.

questions.question: Where did computer-use agents actually stand on the 2026 benchmarks?
options: Solved — frontier models score ~99%
Grounding is nearly solved (ScreenSpot-Pro ~85-90%) but end-to-end workflows are not: VisualWebArena frontier ~27%, and on AgentVista's realistic 12-domain tasks frontier models score only 27-40%
No model exceeds 5% anywhere
Benchmarks for agents don't exist yet
questions.explanation: The gap between near-perfect single-click grounding and ~30% workflow completion is the compounding-error story — and why long-horizon memory, recovery, and visual tool use (ChartAgent-style crop/zoom/OCR calls) are the active frontier.
